title: Resource Status Schema
description: Canonical two-axis status contract for any tracked resource (human, robot, agent, equipment, material, inspection) in the Ectropy PM Core. Ratifies and supersedes the unregistered EB-local DRAFT v0.1.0 (Ectropy-Business .roadmap/schemas/resource-status.schema.json, authored 2026-07-09), which formalized ROS luhtech_msgs/ResourceStatus.msg's single bare `status` string field verbatim -- carrying its conflation of two distinct semantic axes (is-this-resource-reachable/allocatable, vs. what-is-it-doing-right-now) into one flat enum. This contract splits that into availability (reachability/allocation) and activity (current behavior), the same two-axis pattern voxel-group.schema.json already established for rolledUpStatus (work-progress) / rolledUpHealth (risk), including a documented legacy-value migration mapping. Home: /ectropy/resource/ (new category, first landing -- this schema was never actually deployed at the EB-local draft's flat path). Contract per DISPATCH-EB-CLI-FULL-ARC-EXECUTION-TARBALL-2026-07-14 Phase alpha-0.

urn.description: MIRROR of https://schemas.luh.tech/_definitions/graph.schema.json#/definitions/urn, same inline-mirror convention as voxel-group.schema.json#/definitions/urn (public endpoint is fail-closed per SR #185; byte-equality is CI-asserted, not $ref-resolved).

resourceType.description: Resource taxonomy. MIRRORED byte-for-byte in resource-pose.schema.json#/definitions/resourceType (same inline-mirror convention as definitions.urn). Live producers today (uro-sim simulator nodes): human (humsx_simulator_node.py, /hsx/), robot (robsx_simulator_node.py, /rsx/), equipment (eqpsx_simulator_node.py, /esx/). agent/material/inspection are contract-reserved for forward compatibility per Phase alpha-0's field list -- no live uro-sim producer node exists for these today (there is no msx/isx simulator node in the current colcon workspace).

availability.description: Availability axis: is this resource currently reachable/allocatable for new work. Split from the legacy conflated ResourceStatus.msg `status` field. See x-authoring.migration for the legacy-value mapping.

activity.description: Activity axis: what the resource is doing right now, independent of its availability. Split from the legacy conflated ResourceStatus.msg `status` field. See x-authoring.migration for the legacy-value mapping.

$schema.description: Schema declaration. Canonical: https://schemas.luh.tech/ectropy/resource/resource-status.schema.json. Plain string per d-2026-06-22 canon (const antipattern dropped -- which schema governs is schema-refs.json's job, not a self-referential pin). Note: the superseded EB-local DRAFT declared this const; that is corrected here.

resourceUrn.description: Corresponds to ResourceStatus.msg's resource_urn field. Format urn:luhtech:ectropy:resource:{id}.

utilization.description: Corresponds to ResourceStatus.msg's utilization field (float64 in the .msg; constrained here to the documented 0-1 range).

lastUpdate.description: Corresponds to ResourceStatus.msg's last_update field (builtin_interfaces/Time in the .msg).

authoredFrom: voxel-group.schema.json's rolledUpStatus/rolledUpHealth two-axis precedent (definitions + migration-note pattern)
x-authoring.producerOfRecord: humsx_simulator_node.py (/hsx/{id}/status), robsx_simulator_node.py (/rsx/{id}/status), eqpsx_simulator_node.py (/esx/{id}/status) -- all publish the same luhtech_msgs/ResourceStatus message; the ROS .msg is unchanged by this contract (still one bare status string) -- this schema is the typed target the alpha-2 ROS->Redis producer bridge decodes that string INTO, not a replacement wire format.

migration.note: Legacy ResourceStatus.msg status value -> (availability, activity) pair. BREAK maps to AVAILABLE (not OFFLINE/ASSIGNED): a resource on a scheduled break is still reachable and will return, which availability is meant to capture; activity=BREAK carries the distinct at-this instant-not-working meaning.
